summary: Evidence linking cattle collar sync → event bus (NATS) → geo mapping + dashboard/web sync points.

findings.role: Poll collars (multiple vendors), normalize state, publish events
findings.notes: Normalized state includes gps, pastureId, battery, motion and raw vendor payload. Events published to EventBus (NATS).

findings.role: Adapter that fetches vendor-specific collar payloads and shapes them into CollarState

evidence.snippet: async fetchStates(): Promise<CollarState[]> { // TODO: Replace with real Gallagher API integration
gps: { lat: 38.9, lon: -80.2, distanceToBoundary: 15, outsidePasture: false }

findings.role: Publishes infra events to NATS subjects (seven.events.<source>.<type>) and seven.events.all
findings.notes: Any system that subscribes to NATS subjects (websocket bridge, dashboard, external systems) can receive collar events in real-time.

findings.role: Local handler registry that wires EventBus events to internal handlers

evidence.snippet: async dispatch(event: InfraEvent) { const handlers = this.getHandlers(event.type); ... }

findings.role: Resolve GPS coordinates to region/subregion/parcel/gridCell used by dashboards and overlays

evidence.snippet: const { lat: nLat, lon: nLon } = GeoNormalize.normalize(lat, lon);

findings.notes: GeoMapping.resolve is referenced by geo-context and backend overlay code; dashboards use ctx.location.gridCell to render overlays.

findings.role: Consume location.gridCell from seven-runtime context for visual overlays
Example bridge showing bus.subscribe -> sevenHandleEvent(JSON.parse(msg)) — indicates runtime subscribes and routes external events
findings.notes: This file demonstrates how NATS topics are consumed and handed to runtime handlers; a similar subscription can power websocket or HTTP push to web apps.

findings.role: Handles offline/resilient delivery of critical events when ground networks are down

evidence.snippet: enterSatelliteOnly(reason: string) { this.status = { mode: 'SATELLITE_ONLY' ... }; this.speak('Satellite continuity mode engaged. Ground systems unavailable.'); }
async flushCritical(events: QueuedEvent[]) { const sorted = SyncPriority.sort(events); for (const event of sorted) { console.log('[Satellite Continuity] Queued critical event:', event.type); } }

findings.notes: Useful to ensure collar critical events are not lost during connectivity outages; indicates design for reliable sync to external systems.

conclusion: Cattle collar data flows: vendor adapters -> collar-collar-sync -> normalize -> publishEvent -> EventBus (NATS) -> subscribers/dispatchers -> geo-context/overlays/dashboards. Web app sync is supported indirectly via NATS subscriptions or a WebSocket bridge; GeoMapping.resolve is used to map GPS to gridCell for overlays.
recommendations: Deploy a NATS-to-WebSocket bridge or lightweight WebSocket server subscribed to seven.events.* to push CATTLE_STATE_UPDATED events to browser dashboards in real-time.
Ensure GeoMapping.normalize is applied before publishing to public dashboards to avoid leaking raw coordinates if privacy is required (publish regionId/gridCell instead of exact lat/lon).
Add a handler registration for CATTLE_STATE_UPDATED in EventDispatcher to persist recent collar states to a fast store (Redis) used by dashboards for live queries.
Instrument collar vendor adapters (Gallagher, WaveTech, Moovement) with retry/backoff and credential rotation; avoid embedding secrets in code or config files.
